g.text: FIRES. The only error sync sees.
One shared axis, t=0 at inbound webhook acceptance. The wake lane is cut short to fit;
its 120s marker sits where the label starts. The polling TTL (1h) is ~30x past the right
Red fires. Grey is dead code on the sync path.
before the delivery budget can finish even its fastest possible pass (31s), and
`wakeTimeout` (120s) is 4x the deadline. So a sync caller always gets
`504 sync_deadline_exceeded` first, and never observes the other two.
The delivery budget is ~31s at best (every attempt fails fast on a connect error or an
immediate non-2xx) and ~71s at worst (31s of backoff plus 4 attempts each burning the full
10s `agentReadTimeout`). Raising `syncDeliveryDeadline` above the delivery budget is what
makes `delivery_failed` reachable. Async mode applies no deadline, so both grey outcomes
are reachable there as error payloads.
